steps.title: Write the circle equation
steps.content:
A circle with center $(-6,3)$ and radius $10$ uses the standard circle form. Substituting the center and radius gives
$$(x+6)^2+(y-3)^2=100$$

steps.title: Expand the binomials
steps.content:
Expand both binomials before moving terms:
$$(x+6)^2=x^2+12x+36$$
$$(y-3)^2=y^2-6y+9$$
So the equation becomes
$$x^2+12x+36+y^2-6y+9=100$$

steps.title: Move terms to general form
steps.content: Move $100$ to the left side and combine constants: $$x^2+y^2+12x-6y+36+9-100=0$$ $$x^2+y^2+12x-6y-55=0$$ Comparing with $x^2+y^2+ax+by+c=0$, $c=-55$. The answer is -55.
